title: Exostema lineatum
taxonomy: Exostema lineatum is classified in the family Rubiaceae and is native to tropical regions. This flowering plant belongs to the genus Exostema, which comprises a limited number of species. It is often referred to as the 'Wild Jasmine' due to its fragrant flowers. Exostema lineatum is native to Central and South America. It is a popular ornamental plant in many parts of the world due to its vibrant blooms and attractive foliage.
uses: Exostema lineatum, a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's leaves are a source of the alkaloid lineatin, which has shown potential in inhibiting the growth of certain microorganisms. Exostema lineatum has been traditionally used to treat ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its medicinal properties are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Further research is needed to fully explore the potential benefits of Exostema lineatum as a medicinal plant.

intro: Exostema lineatum, also known as the local name 'Palo de agua' in some countries, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
